steps.title: Find social pressure
steps.content: The claim concerns Newland following social conventions dictated by other men in the audience. The best quotation should show the group's standards shaping what Newland accepts.

steps.title: Reject scenery and superiority
steps.content: Choice B describes where Newland looks. Choice C says he feels superior to the men, which does not show him following them. Choice D discusses the opera setting, not social pressure.

steps.title: Use masculine solidarity
steps.content: Choice A says the grouped men represented New York and that masculine solidarity made Newland accept their doctrine, directly illustrating his conformity to their conventions. The answer is A.
